description_long: This object is a vintage desktop computer setup featuring a large CRT monitor with a thick, boxy profile and a slightly curved back. The monitor sits atop a rectangular base unit that likely houses the computer's main components, with a visible floppy disk drive on the front. The screen is framed by a wide, light-colored bezel, and the display area itself is a muted pink. Accompanying the monitor are a full-sized keyboard with beige and gray keys and a matching, rounded mouse. The rear of the monitor has a series of ventilation slits for cooling. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on function over form.
description: A vintage CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, paired with a matching keyboard and mouse. The setup is boxy, light-colored, and features a front-facing floppy disk drive.
description_view_0: Side view showing the deep, curved back of the CRT monitor, the rectangular base, and the keyboard and mouse positioned to the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide bezel, pink display area, floppy disk drive, and the full keyboard and mouse directly in front.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the monitor's depth, rear ventilation slits, and the keyboard and mouse to the left.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the monitor's ventilation slits and the back of the base unit, with the keyboard and mouse partially visible.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is wide, flat, and made of matte beige plastic, with squared corners and no visible decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a rectangular, integrated base unit that is boxy and solid, matching the monitor's color and material, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: A small group of buttons is visible on the lower left of the base unit, aligned horizontally below the screen.
